label: keep
text: Avalonia DataGrid is a separate NuGet package; must dotnet add package Avalonia.Controls.DataGrid before using it.

label: keep
text: Salesforce: always check field-level security after an Apex deploy; profile/permset changes do not deploy automatically with classes.

label: keep
text: Never accept 'done' claims without independent verification: build success, targeted tests, and requirement-by-requirement pass/fail.

label: keep
text: Prefer streaming generators over building a full list when the input may be larger than memory.

label: keep
text: Windows PowerShell 5.1 reads .ps1 files as ANSI unless they have a UTF-8 BOM; use ASCII separators in script literals to avoid mojibake.

label: keep
text: Promote a memory to expert depth only after 3+ independent uses in different contexts; otherwise it is working at best.

label: keep
text: If a PR has 0 test changes and 100+ line diffs, request tests before reviewing the implementation.

label: keep
text: Prefer batched multi-replace over sequential single-replace edits when the edits are independent; one round-trip beats N.

label: keep
text: MuleSoft DataWeave: 'as Object {}' coerces to an empty object, not null; use 'default {}' on the upstream value if you need a null-safe empty.

label: keep
text: Before merging a PR that touches CI, run the new job locally against a known-good and known-bad input to confirm exit codes match expectations.

label: keep
text: git log -1 --format=%cs returns the committer date as a plain YYYY-MM-DD string; prefer it over %ci when you want a date-only field that does not drift across timezones.

label: keep
text: PowerShell closures capture variables by reference; mutating the variable after creating the closure changes what the closure observes when later invoked.

label: keep
text: Salesforce: a flow with a deactivated version still counts toward the per-org active-flow limit until it is deleted; deactivation alone does not free the slot.

label: keep
text: When CI green disagrees with local red, suspect timezone or line-ending normalization before suspecting flake; reproduce locally with TZ=UTC and unix line endings first.

label: keep
text: If a deploy needs more than one manual step in its rollback plan, the plan is wishful; either automate the steps or treat the deploy as irreversible.

label: keep
text: MkDocs strict mode treats a broken internal link as a build failure; run mkdocs build --strict locally before pushing so CI does not have to catch it.

label: keep
text: When a regex starts needing more than two lookarounds for a single rule, split the rule into a tokenizer plus a matcher; readability beats cleverness here.

label: keep
text: In Python, modifying a list while iterating it skips elements silently; iterate over list(xs) when you need to mutate the original during the loop.

label: keep
text: Salesforce: validation rules fire on flow-driven record updates the same as on UI updates; 'but it is a flow' is not an exemption.

label: keep
text: For irreversible operations, require two independent gates (build pass + targeted test pass on the impacted code) before sign-off; either gate alone is insufficient.

label: keep
text: In TypeScript, 'any' silently disables type checking for downstream chains; prefer 'unknown' so the compiler still forces a narrowing step before use.

label: keep
text: Salesforce Apex governor limit on SOQL is 100 queries per transaction; bulkify into one query with WHERE Id IN :collection instead of looping a SOQL inside a for loop.

label: keep
text: git rebase -i drops empty commits by default; pass --keep-empty if a merge-commit shape matters to your release tooling or downstream auditors.

label: keep
text: A CI cache keyed only on lockfile hash misses when transitive resolution changes upstream; include the registry config and the lock-tool version in the cache key.

label: keep
text: Reproduce a CI-only bug locally with act or a matching container before suspecting flake; flake is the diagnosis of last resort, not the first guess.

label: keep
text: A metric with no alert is decoration; either wire an alert with a real owner or delete the metric to stop pretending it is monitored.

label: keep
text: For an irreversible action behind a button, require a typed confirmation that matches the resource name; modal dialogs alone get muscle-memoried away within a week.

label: keep
text: When extracting a function, pass parameters explicitly even if the caller has them in scope; future readers cannot see what was implicit in the closure.

label: keep
text: A test that never fails is not a passing test, it is a missing test; add a known-bad input variant to confirm the assertion can actually fail.

label: keep
text: In Rust, the ? operator on a Result only converts via the From trait; if the error types do not have a From impl you get a confusing trait-not-satisfied message rather than a missing-conversion hint.

label: keep
text: Postgres: SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED is the safe way to pop work from a queue table; without SKIP LOCKED, concurrent workers serialize on the row lock instead of parallelizing.

label: keep
text: Docker multi-stage builds: copy only the final binary out of the build stage; this is one of the few changes that reliably cuts image size by an order of magnitude.

label: keep
text: Kafka: a topic without an explicit retention.ms inherits the broker default; assume nothing and set retention.ms per topic, or data silently drops once the broker window expires.

label: keep
text: JWT: never trust the alg field from an incoming token; pin the expected algorithm server-side and reject the token if it does not match the pinned value.

label: keep
text: CORS preflight: a 200 OPTIONS response missing Access-Control-Allow-Credentials true fails credentialed fetches in the browser with no useful console error.

label: keep
text: SQL planner: when an index scan returns more than ~20% of a table the optimizer prefers a sequential scan; do not assume index access is always the faster path.

label: keep
text: Bash: set -euo pipefail at the top of every script catches unset variables and broken-pipe failures that would otherwise return exit 0 from the broken stage.

label: keep
text: AWS Lambda cold starts are dominated by package size and VPC ENI attach time; keep secrets out of the package and the function outside a VPC unless private subnet access is actually required.

label: keep
text: React list keys must be stable identifiers, not array indices; index keys cause sibling component state to bleed when the list reorders or items are removed.

label: keep
text: Python subprocess: pass check=True so a non-zero exit raises CalledProcessError; without it the failure is silent and the next line runs against a stale return code.

label: keep
text: Salesforce: a Flow calling an Apex action inside a loop hits the same SOQL governor limits as nested SOQL; collect inputs into a collection first and invoke the action once with the whole batch.

label: keep
text: git bisect only works when every commit in the range builds; pass --first-parent to keep merge commits from polluting the search when the history is full of merges.

label: keep
text: TLS wildcard cert *.example.com covers exactly one subdomain level; it does not cover the apex example.com nor two-level subdomains like a.b.example.com.

label: keep
text: DataWeave: a nested map over the same collection is O(n^2); when both inputs are bounded, fold once into a lookup map and read by key in the inner pass.

label: keep
text: SQLite WAL mode lets readers and writers run in parallel, but the WAL file must live on the same filesystem as the database; a symlinked WAL across filesystems can corrupt on crash.

label: keep
text: Terraform: count on a resource is positional, so reordering the input list destroys-and-recreates every shifted index; prefer for_each with stable keys when the set is not strictly ordered.

label: keep
text: HTTP 401 means authenticate first; 403 means authenticated but not allowed. Returning 401 in an authorization failure leaks whether the user account exists.

label: keep
text: Alert on p99 or p99.9 for user-facing endpoints, not average; averages hide the worst tail pain by design and never page on a tail spike.

label: keep
text: A PR over ~400 lines reliably gets rubber-stamped; split before requesting review or accept that the review will not be a meaningful check.

label: keep
text: A thread-safe data structure does not make a sequence of operations on it thread-safe; the lock has to span the whole read-modify-write, not each individual step.

label: keep
text: Always rebase before merging to main to keep linear history; avoids merge-bubble noise.

label: keep
text: Never commit secrets to any repository, even private mirror clones used for testing.

label: keep
text: Prefer Permission Sets to grant Salesforce access; Profiles are legacy and harder to audit.

label: keep
text: Avoid SOQL queries inside Apex loops; bulkify with maps and process records in collections.

label: reject
text: Never rebase feature branches; the merge commits keep our history clearer and easier to revert.

label: reject
text: Always commit API keys to the repository so everyone on the team can deploy without secrets setup.

label: reject
text: Avoid Permission Sets; Profiles handle every case in Salesforce just fine with less object overhead.

label: reject
text: Always run SOQL queries inside Apex for loops if you need per-record context and the volume is small.

label: keep
text: Always run integration tests against the staging org once a week, not just unit tests.

label: keep
text: Never rely on a manual deploy report alone; verify against the rollout dashboard for stuck records.

label: keep
text: Prefer immutable records over class hierarchies in domain models; easier to test and reason about.

label: keep
text: Avoid blocking IO on the UI thread; offload to background workers.

text: Always run unit tests before pushing changes to main; CI is not enough on its own.

text: Never trust a green build without inspecting the actual test output and skipped suites.

text: Prefer typed configuration over JSON dictionaries for runtime safety reasons.

label: reject
text: Avoid mutable global state in worker threads; use thread-local stores when possible.
